summary: This Jummah (Friday) sermon at the Muslim Community Center of Tucson focuses on self-critique and accountability as essential Islamic practices. The speaker emphasizes three types of inner selves (nafs): the commanding self (that incites evil), the blaming self (that critiques), and the tranquil self (at peace with Allah). The sermon explains how self-examination helps believers recognize their shortcomings, correct mistakes, and improve spiritually. The speaker illustrates these concepts through the famous story of a murderer who killed 99 people and sought repentance, demonstrating how sincere self-reflection and intention to return to Allah can lead to forgiveness and paradise.
suggested_title: Self-Critique and Repentance: Understanding the Three Types of Inner Self (Nafs)
catchy_title: Can a Murderer Reach Paradise? The Path to Repentance
